title: Gate evidence bundle (v0.4)
description: One readable JSON document binding per-stage in-toto-shaped Statement entries to content-addressed subjects at the bundle top level: the PR head commit plus one sha256 digest per ingested input file. Contract v0.4 — v0 (LOCKED — resolution of TzolkinB/skills#102) plus an additive `EMPTY` execution result (TzolkinB/skills#111, v0.1, ADR-0031) plus the internal witness:// -> gate:// identifier rename (TzolkinB/skills#113, v0.2, ADR-0033) plus the proven -> confirmed taxonomy rename (TzolkinB/skills#126, v0.3, ADR-0034) plus per-input sha256 subjects binding the decision to the exact ingested bytes (TzolkinB/skills#139, v0.4, ADR-0037 §2) — additive only, no shape change to `resourceDescriptor`. Backward-compatible with v0.3: a bundle with only the `pr-head` subject (no input digests) still validates, since `subject` only requires ≥1 entry. Execution evidence = Playwright JSON and/or a Cypress Module API result (added additively per contract Q1: `stage` is a free string, so a new framework needs no schema change or version bump); no numeric confidence. audit-test rides either OPAQUE (Markdown inline, caps at canary) or PARSED (verdict.result+label — the B→A graduation, TzolkinB/skills#49; PASSED+confirmed unlocks ship). Extensibility = versioned predicateType URIs + ignore-unrecognized + per-entry annotations.

subject.description: The bundle's content-addressed subjects (contract Q4): always the `pr-head` git-commit subject, plus one sha256-digest subject per ingested input file (TzolkinB/skills#139) — swap an input's bytes and its digest, and so its subject, changes. Digests are lowercase hex strings living here, never in the gate predicate (honesty guard #3 is unaffected). Per-entry subject is reserved for future cross-run aggregation.

producer.description: SLSA builder/metadata — who produced this evidence and when.

statement.description: An in-toto-shaped Statement (ADR-0032) carrying one stage's evidence — not a signed attestation (kept readable — no DSSE/base64 in v0).

evidencePredicate.description: A stage's evidence. `stage` is a data field (contract Q1), so new stages need no schema change.

verdict.comment: verdict.confidence is CUT in v0 (contract Q6). Its arrival is the signal Gate has earned a number — it requires a schemaVersion bump.

gatePredicate.description: The derived gate decision (gate-function spec v0, LOCKED — #103). Honesty guard #3: additionalProperties:false and NO numeric field anywhere — a validator rejects a smuggled number, so re-adding confidence forces a schemaVersion bump.
